finding: 4-node repro: {Camera (factoryID=23) + 'Frame B' framing behavior (factoryID=3) + 'Source' layer + 'Transition B' scenenode (factoryID=11)}. All 14 Replicator/Cell tiles were REMOVABLE without losing the bug -> the divergence is NOT the tile wall, it is the FRAMED-CAMERA POSE of a single framed layer (confirms T-H2/S6 resolveFramedWallPose anchor bug). Per-frame engine-vs-FCP: f00-f07 both black (match); f08-f19 FCP=black(0.0) but ENGINE renders B at mean 34.3 (engine brings framed B on-screen ~12 frames too early, at a wrong static pose); f20-f23 FCP reveals B (mean 51->119, the real Frame-B framing ramp) while ENGINE stays FROZEN at 34.3 (mis-posed, static, never matches the reveal). Net: engine paints one static off-centre framed 'Transition B' the whole clip instead of FCP's black-then-framed-reveal schedule. Bug lives in the framing pose/schedule for the 'Frame B' behavior (factoryID=3) + Camera, i.e. evaluator/framing.ts resolveFramedWallPose anchor (bottom-edge [2399,-2145] instead of content centroid) AND its reveal timing, not the replicator grid.
